zh.Cheadle, Staffordshire, England: 英國斯塔福爾黑阿德爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Savage於1332年出生在英國斯塔福爾黑阿德爾時，她的父親Robert Savage28歲，母親Amicia de Walkington27歲。她與Ralph de St Leger在英國肯特武爾可姆結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1359年4月4日之前在英國英格蘭肯特武爾可姆，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特武爾可姆。

zh-hans.Cheadle, Staffordshire, England: 英国斯塔福爾黑阿德爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Savage于1332年出生在英国斯塔福爾黑阿德爾时，她的父亲Robert Savage28岁，母亲Amicia de Walkington27岁。她与Ralph de St Leger在英国肯特武爾可姆结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1359年4月4日之前在英国英格兰肯特武爾可姆，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特武爾可姆。

nameMeanings.name: Savage
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Middle English and Old French salvage, sauvage ‘wild, untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, literally ‘man of the woods’, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’ influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). Compare French Sauvage .

Irish (Down): generally of English origin (it was taken to County Down in the 12th century), this name has also sometimes been adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Ó Sabháin, the name of a small south Munster sept, which was earlier Anglicized as O'Savin (see Savin ).
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic) Savich and of Serbian Savić (see Savic ).

variants.name: Savitt
meanings: Possibly English or Irish: variant of   Savage  . This surname is very rare in Britain.
Alternatively, perhaps an Americanized form of Jewish   Savich   or an American shortened and altered form of Jewish   Savitski  .

variants.name: Sauvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Armand, Jean-Jacques.

French and Channel Islands: nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Old French salvage, sauvage ‘untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’, influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). This surname is also found in England.
History: Jean Sauvage from Plouëc-du-Trieux in Côtes-d'Armor, Brittany (France), married Marguerite Martin in Les Cèdres, QC, in 1782.

variants.name: Salvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique.
English (Sussex): variant of   Savage  .
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

variants.name: Ramage
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin); French: nickname for a savage or unpredictable individual, from Old French ramage ‘wild, untamed’. The word is recorded once in Middle English with the sense ‘courageous’ and in Scots (spelled rammage) as ‘rash, furious’. Compare Savage .

variants.name: Sabin
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Northamptonshire): from the Middle English personal name Sabin (from Latin Sabinus; see 2 below) or its female equivalent Sabine (from Latin Sabina). In medieval England the feminine form was always more popular as a personal name.

French: from the Old French personal name Sabin, from Latin Sabinus. The name was originally referred to the Sabines, an ancient Italic people of central Italy whose name is of uncertain origin. In the 8th century BC the Romans slaughtered the Sabine menfolk and carried off the women. More influential as far as name-giving is concerned was the existence of several early Christian saints named Sabinus.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Sabháin ‘descendant of Sabhán’, a personal name based on sabh ‘cub’. As an Irish surname, this has mostly been absorbed into Savage .

variants.name: Selvidge
meanings: English (Leicestershire):
from Middle English selvage, silvadge ‘edge of a cloth, bandage, ornamental edging (to a cloth or garment)’, hence a nickname for someone who made or sold such items.
variant of   Savage  .

variants.name: Savidge
meanings: English: variant of   Savage  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Savage was born in 1332, in Cheadle, Staffordshire, England, her father, Robert Savage, was 28 and her mother, Amicia de Walkington, was 27. She married Ralph de St Leger in Ulcombe, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died before 4 April 1359, in Ulcombe, Kent, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Ulcombe, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Savage est née en 1332, à Cheadle, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert Savage, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Amicia de Walkington, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Ralph de St Leger à Ulcombe, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée avant 4 avril 1359, à Ulcombe, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Ulcombe, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Savage 1332 in Cheadle, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert Savage 28 und ihre Mutter Amicia de Walkington 27. Sie hat in Ulcombe, Kent, England Ralph de St Leger geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb vor 4. April 1359 in Ulcombe, Kent, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Ulcombe, Kent, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Savage è nata il nel 1332 nel Cheadle, Staffordshire, England, sua padre, Robert Savage, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Amicia de Walkington, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Ralph de St Leger nel Ulcombe, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì prima del 4 aprile 1359, nel Ulcombe, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolta a Ulcombe, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.Ulcombe, Kent, England: イギリスケントウルコンベ
ja.Cheadle, Staffordshire, England: イギリススタフォードシュアイアチェアドル
ja.lifeSummary: Joan Savageが1332年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアチェアドルで生まれた時，父Robert Savageは28歳，母Amicia de Walkingtonは27歳であった。イギリスケントウルコンベでRalph de St Legerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1359年4月4日以前にイギリスイギリスケントウルコンベで死去し，イギリスイギリスケントウルコンベにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Savage(이)가 1332년 영국 스태퍼드실르 체아들일에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Savage(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Amicia de Walkington(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 영국 켄트 울코브에서 Ralph de St Leger(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1359년 4월 4일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 울코브에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 울코브에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Savage nasceu em 1332, em Cheadle, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert Savage, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Amicia de Walkington, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Ralph de St Leger em Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 4 de abril de 1359, em Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultada em Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Savage родилась в 1332 г. в местности чидл, стаффордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert Savage было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Amicia de Walkington, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ralph de St Leger в местности улкомб, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла перед 4 апрель 1359 г. в местности улкомб, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности улкомб, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Savage nació en 1332, en Cheadle, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert Savage, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Amicia de Walkington, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Ralph de St Leger en Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió antes de 4 de abril de 1359, en Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultada en Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
